hints.text: Plates white + **blue-yellow strip left** (flag colours; old plates: strip hard to see)

shared_with.note: Russian plates fully white, NO strip.

hints.text: All coverage **Gen3**, mostly a **RED Google car** (± antenna; colour sometimes visible via blur glitch)
hints.uniq_note: The red Gen3 car is almost unique to Ukraine (parts of Belgium have a red long-antenna car). Vs Russia: no red car there, Russia has Gen4/Gen2 (Ukraine never), Russian Gen3 always has an antenna — antenna-less = think Ukraine.

hints.text: Rarer cars: black long-antenna, white long-antenna, grey short-antenna (regional — see below)
Some of **Europe's worst roads** — potholes, cracks, warped pavement (Russia's are generally better)
Random roadside objects (esp. fences) painted **blue-and-yellow**
**Ukrainian Cyrillic**: unique letters **Є Ґ І Ї** (vs Russian); street = **вулиця (вул.)**, city = **місто (м.)** (Russian: улица/ул., город/г.)

shared_with.note: Russian Cyrillic base looks the same.
shared_with.diff: **і/ї/є** or вул. → Ukraine; Ы/Э + ул. → Russia.

shared_with.note: Belarusian Cyrillic also uses і — plus **ў**.

hints.text: Square concrete poles (varied tops, white-painted bottoms) + **diagonal support beams**; wooden poles w/ concrete base blocks — post-Soviet kit (RU/LT similar)

shared_with.diff: Diagonal beams commoner in UA; plates/language decide.

hints.text: Chevrons **red w/ white arrows** (same in RU/EE); pedestrian signs **3 stripes** (also RU/EE/LT); white town-entry signs; **B&W-painted guardrails** (like Russia)
Soviet apartment blocks of **light-grey brick, rundown** (Baltic versions better kept); suburbs = fenced brick single-family houses, heavy vegetation, unpaved roads, corrugated roofs

**Rural vibe**: bad road + flat/slightly hilly + tree line then open fields + no infrastructure + no traffic; almost all coverage = **summer**; tall **poplars** common (most of Russia lacks them); ultra-rural tracks near cities exist despite the urban+main-road coverage rule
**Coverage map**: mostly cities + yellow roads; almost nothing in the SE (except Donetsk) and south of Odesa near Romania
**Area codes** per oblast (3rd digit 2 = oblast capital); **plate letter codes = oblast** (non-intuitive, learnable); oblast **second-level domains** (.te.ua = Ternopil) mostly intuitive

**May coverage** (yellowish leaf tint) → western half; **2011 autumn coverage** in 5 cities: Kyiv/Lviv/Kharkiv (mostly overwritten, white car), **Odesa** (mostly intact, silver short-antenna car), **Donetsk** (ONLY 2011, black long-antenna car — red car = never Donetsk)

**Carpathians** (west): hilly-mountainous, forested, pines, curvy roads; **Baltic-pine forests** → north near Belarus (flat!); **south** = warmer, drier, shrubs + open fields; blooming **sunflowers** → far south + east (Kherson/Mykolaiv/Odesa; T0403 to Nova Kakhovka)
**Russian language** → east + south (Ы/Э, ул., г.)

**Car metas**: leaf on the car's back → Ternopil↔Zolochiv/Brody/Dubno roads (H02/P39/E40/P26/E85); visual glitch → only Ivano-Frankivsk; yellow **Opel flag on the antenna** → one side of the Chernobyl road
**Red soil** on mine tracks + Brazil-style poles (bait! foliage isn't tropical + long antenna); poles w/ **red band** unique to Kryvyi Rih
**Sunflower bus shelters** (yellow-green, 'Оліс' brand) → Mykolaiv + Kherson oblasts; **'Polish' bollards** (wrap-around red reflector) → Zakarpattia
**Hungarian border area**: bilingual/monolingual Hungarian signs + Hungarian-style linear villages (single-storey, orange or diamond-slate roofs)
Special car trekker Ivankiv → **Pripyat**: inside Pripyat = abandoned, overgrown, eerie buildings
